description: Mechanical anti-repetition rules enforced by validate.py Check 13/14. Added 2026-04-23 after the engagement-review honesty pass surfaced that 11+ stories had used the same opener stamp.

rules.rationale_consecutive_opener: If the first 8 characters of sentence 0 (after stripping leading numerics) appear identically as the first 8 characters of sentence 0 in any of the previous 3 stories, the story is rejected. Forces opener variety mechanically because the human reviewer demonstrably stopped enforcing pre-check item 5.
rules.rationale_grandfather: Stories with story_id <= 67 were authored before this guardrail existed; Check 13 and Check 14 emit warnings (not errors) for them so the existing library still ships while the back-catalog is rewritten. New stories (id >= 68) get hard errors. When the failing stories are rewritten, lower this number.
rules.rationale_title_grammar: If the story's new_grammar introduces a form (e.g. masen_deshita = 待ちませんでした) and the title's literal JP text is exactly that surface form (e.g. '待ちませんでした'), the story is rejected. authoring_rules.md §6 warns against letting the new grammar drive the plot; titling the story after the form is the most visible symptom.
rules.rationale_library_opener_blocklist: These exact 6-7-character openers have been used too many times in the existing library to use again. New stories must find a different opening shape. Removing an item from this list requires a justification comment in this file.
